channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How Apple could work with DeepSeek to pull ahead in the AI race
item.description: Ever since DeepSeek burst onto the scene last month, there’s been no shortage of opinions about what the Chinese startup’s artificial intelligence accomplishments mean for America’s AI giants like OpenAI, Microsoft, Google, and Meta.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Super Bowl 2025 LIVE: Eagles vs Chiefs stream, watch the NFL Championship game, halftime show and pre-game news
item.description: Eager to watch the Super Bowl? Here are the best streams, the cheapest prices and how to watch all four quarters live online as Patrick Mahomes' Kansas City face Philadelphia at New Orleans' Superdome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sony PlayStation Network outage enrages gamers around the world
item.description: Sony’s PlayStation Network (PSN) is experiencing a major outage across the world, leaving tens of thousands of gamers unable to access online services, stores and multimedia apps.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This app sends faxes for free from your iPhone, Android device, Mac, or PC
item.description: Nowadays, when you hear someone talk about faxing, there’s a decent chance it’s the punchline to a groan-inducing dad joke. (Not that I would ever be guilty of such silliness, of course. I stick strictly to the fax.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: PSN is down – gamers left without their online fix as PlayStation Network outage is still ongoing after 12 hours
item.description: We’re seeing red lights across Sony’s service status dashboard, and it’s not looking too good for weekend gaming prospects right now.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Identify any mystery caller for free with this phone number lookup tool
item.description: The TruePeopleSearch.com website works for landline and cellphone numbers, allowing you to do a reverse phone lookup for any type of phone number.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: President Donald Trump’s order declaring diversity, equity and inclusion policies illegal has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Terry Chea And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk’s crew descends on CFPB with plan to shut it down
item.description: The CFPB is funded independently through the Federal Reserve, but the CFPB director has the power to determine how much money the agency needs. That means a CFPB director could zero out the agency's budget entirely.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 has entered the release candidate stage. The next version of standard Java will ship with 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency. The first JDK 24 release candidate arrived February 6, following two rampdown stages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk pledges to rehire young DOGE member after recent racist posts
item.description: The work DOGE has drawn fury from Democrats and charges that it is illegally accessing the private data of millions of Americans, and violating the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: AI lifts workforces to new heights of efficiency and innovation
item.description: AI is a highly effective, multifaceted tool that can not only improve worker productivity for simple tasks, but can also dramatically transform customer service, cybersecurity, and even business strategy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Amazon wants to spend $100 billion this year for ‘once in a lifetime' AI opportunity
item.description: Amazon says it expects to spend in excess of $100 billion this year, with the “vast majority” going straight into AI projects.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Amazon's Android TV update hints at new Fire TV devices – maybe at the next-gen AI Alexa event soon
item.description: It looks like Amazon is plowing ahead with new Fire TV devices for Android 14, and we might see them soon.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Severance director Ben Stiller says work is underway on season 3's scripts – and he hopes it won't take three years to make
item.description: Apple hasn't renewed Severance for a third season yet, but its creative team is already hard at work on developing it.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: UK orders Apple to let it access everyone’s encrypted data
item.description: In its limited wisdom, the deeply unpopular UK government has decided to break privacy for the entire world, slamming Apple with a top secret order that demands blanket access to personal data. Apple must create a “back door” to enable surveillance, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Honeywell se transforma a partir de la IA generativa
item.description: Tras haber pasado años transformándose en una empresa totalmente basada en datos, el conglomerado estadounidense Honeywell está ahora preparado para aprovechar la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa en todas las facetas de su negocio para impulsar la mejora de la productividad, la colaboración y
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia Scaling Back RTX 4060 Production: What it Means for You
item.description: Nvidia is reducing production of the popular RTX 4060 graphics card, likely due to the upcoming launch of its successor, the RTX 5060.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Windows PowerToys: Your handy productivity toolbox
item.description: Back in the 1990s, Windows power users often availed themselves of a set of small, free software tools from Microsoft collectively known as “PowerToys.” (Read about their history at Wikipedia.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Repsol impulsa su estrategia digital con la incorporación de agentes de IA
item.description: Repsol ha anunciado la ampliación del acuerdo de colaboración suscrito con Accenture con el fin de continuar avanzando hacia los objetivos de su Programa Digital.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Full-stack JavaScript leads the way
item.description: Building web apps begins with a simple premise, but the real-world experience is a juggling act of optimizations, best practices, toolchains, and so on. Developers tend to respond by doing what we do best: creating new tools and techniques to manage ever-changing demands.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The number of venomous snakes removed from this Australian yard will make you shudder
item.description: A man described feeling “the shudders” as more than 100 venomous red-bellied black snakes were removed from a pile of mulch in his Sydney backyard.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Like TikTok, lawmakers want to ban DeepSeek from government devices
item.description: A bipartisan duo in the the U.S. House is proposing legislation to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek from federal devices, similar to the policy already in place for the popular social media platform TikTok. Reps. Josh Gottheimer, D-N.J., and Darin LaHood, R-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, PIX 11
